profiling_summary: Hotspots: MPI_Waitall 40%, message exchange dominates runtime, communication latency is the primary bottleneck.

ranked_candidates.target: MPI runtime/library selection and version for 2-rank ping-pong
ranked_candidates.rationale: Profiling shows communication wait dominates (MPI_Waitall/latency bottleneck); switching to a newer or better-tuned MPI stack (and enabling its transport features) can reduce latency without changing message matching semantics.
ranked_candidates.action_sketch: Benchmark the same binary across multiple MPI implementations/versions available on the system (e.g., newer Open MPI vs newer MPICH/vendor MPI) and select the one with lowest ping-pong time; ensure consistent transport configuration (e.g., UCX/OFI provider) and rerun with identical rank/tag/comm/order.
preconditions: Access to multiple MPI implementations/versions or ability to load different MPI modules/containers on the target cluster
Rebuild the application with each MPI (ABI compatibility not assumed) using the same compiler and optimization level
Network fabric and job launcher support the chosen MPI transport/provider
correctness_checks: CHECKSUM output unchanged for same msg_size/iters across variants
No MPI errors; strict preservation of rank/tag/comm/order message matching (no code changes)
Bitwise buffer initialization pattern unchanged (sanity: checksum consistent across repeated runs within same variant)
rollback_criteria: TIME_SEC regresses by >5% versus baseline under same placement and fabric conditions
CHECKSUM differs or any MPI runtime instability/abort occurs
Observed jitter/variance increases materially (e.g., stddev TIME_SEC >2x baseline)
